title: Roasted Egyptian Lamb Wrap
description: A charred and fluffy Egyptian dish featuring vinegar and leaf wrap steam, made with whole30 friendly ingredients. This nostalgic recipe brings the flavors of Egypt to your table.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, apple cider vinegar, garlic, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Rub the mixture all over the lamb shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Place the lamb shoulder on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes per pound, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the collard green leaves by blanching them in boiling water for 30 seconds.
Remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it thinly.
To assemble the wraps, place a few slices of the lamb onto a collard green leaf, and serve immediately.
Use the leftover lamb and juices to make additional wraps, and store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
